evidence.role: Current employee - Cyber Systems Security Engineer
evidence.text: Excellent pay and benefits packages Good pay and benefits but annoying bureaucracy
Benefits are good and pay is decent. Great benefits, good pay

Very flexible working, really good at trusting their people to work autonomously, as long as you put in your hours and get everything done they are pretty relaxed about when and where you do it, creates a good culture. Compensation is very competitive, good pay, decent pension, plus private health with lots of options to get extra benefits for minimal cut from salary. Decent company, good people, good benefits
Pays for two trainings a year. Fantastic benefits: 401(k), Dental, Vision, HSA, Life Insurance, etc. Great Company Benefits

10% employer contributions to 401k if employee contributes 8%. 6% if employee contributes 0%. Flexibility with remote work where it makes sense. Leadership is pushed to focus on employee careers. Flexible, good pay, great benefits
Processes are well-established, work life balance is great Great work-life balance, very bureaucratic
Good Work Life Balance Yea Good Work Life Balance

They care about work life balance and try to maintain it. Only place in sillicon valley that does. Job is the definition of stable. Easy to move laterally to gain experience or try something new. Great job to learn skills. WORK LIFE BALANCE
Work life balance , 401k match Work life balance
flexible scheduling - 4x10, 9x80, flex etc great benefits understanding management Work Life Balance

Exceptional colleagues: The technical talent and dedication of the people here is truly impressive. I work alongside some of the brightest minds in aerospace and defense Meaningful work: Contributing to national security and cutting-edge technology provides genuine purpose Job security: Stable employment Learning opportunities: Exposure to complex, challenging projects across multiple domains Great People, Disappoin...

Compensation and Benefits: Lockheed Martin provides competitive salaries and comprehensive benefits, including health insurance and educational assistance. â Professional Development: The company supports career growth through training programs and opportunities for advancement. Lockheed Martin

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Software Engineer, Devops
evidence.text: Great company with great manager that believes in training and growing their employees. Many options to move around in the company to learn new things Growth opportunity and flexibility

If you find the right team and right manager, areas for growth in this position are sky-high. I have been on great teams and not-so-great teams, and it boils down to the culture of the team and how willing they are to help out with teaching, so try to assess that before starting as a test engineer here. Most teams are very helpful with career growth though! Great Job with the Right Team
Very laid back and lots of learning opportunities for early career Good resume builder
Friendly coworker. Collaborative work environment Great culture
Really interesting projects. You get to use what you went to school for. Relaxed work environment. Flexible schedule LM Aero FTW
Supportive environment and understanding colleagues N/A
Great coworkers, good leadership, interesting and engaging work. Great work environment
Managementâs amazing work load is manageable Amazing work environment
benefits. onsite clinic. pretty views unethical and toxic
Job security is pretty good Job security

+Everyone here is very friendly and very nice. No toxic culture at all +Everyone will always say they are underpaid - for most positions in the lower cost of living areas, LM definitely pays well. +401k is INSANE. Unfortunately, if you join now, it is not as good anymore, but still way better than most companies. +You will rarely work more than 40 hours a week, and if you ever do, you can just leave early the week a...

Management cares about growth of employees You're on the cutting edge of innovation in a company that's investing heavily into it Very talented higher-level engineers makes for great mentorship 4x10 work week Job security Outstanding experience

-Management cares about growth of employees -Talented higher-level engineers makes for great mentorship -You're on the cutting edge of AI innovation at a huge company that is investing into it heavily -4 days x 10 hour work week -Job security Outstanding experience
paid time off low pay Low pay
Low salary Sometimes work can be slow Good, but low pay

Former department played favourites and it negatively affected the team as a whole. Morale was in the toilet because of them and probably still is. Even other departments treaded lightly as to not upset them if employees wanted to move around/outside of that department. They didn't give promotions due to sick leave (illegal) or because they felt there was a line-up for that position (when in reality, they likely did...
Stressful task with tight deadlines Systems engineer

The workload placed on employees is completely unsustainable. Itâs not possible to stay on top of the workload and manage a portfolio in a reasonable 40 hours. People - including managers - contact employees at all hours. During dinner, while on PTO, over the weekend, when weâre supposed to have this mythical âoff-Fridayâ we earn for working a 4x10 schedule. So many employees I talk to are expressing the sam...
Can't thank of any at the moment. Engineering Aide

LOTS of office politics in some groups. You need to watch your back QUITE a lot in some groups and document everything as people tend to backstab when given the chance Great company if you want a cushy job
Work life balance is not the best but itâs worth it Pretty good
can't really think of any Overall good and caring towards employees!
- Management practices not the best - Does not offer stock - slow pay Outdated

- HR fabricates stories to align to a narrative instead of facts. - immature leadership and toxic, hostile leadership. - subject matter experts act like adult toddlers. - inconsistent and unethical decision making. - lots of hiding behind company policy, and newer managers love to hide behind HR. - highly inefficient operations. - highly inappropriate HR communication, and extremely disruptive and disrespectful HR....
Toxic management, favoritism, lack of promotion routes It's ok
Too big to care about anything. No loyalty. No accountability, hide behind might/size. They Always Forget Who Worked for Them...

Security is toxic and are always chastising LMCO's employees. They put the fear into people working there. If you enjoy your privacy, just forget about it because there is none. Amazing people but ...

evidence.location: Ft. Worth Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base
evidence.text: Stressful work environment, bureaucratic environment lots of favoritism and politics Program Management
Extremely poor management. No growth opportunity. Relationship valued over contribution. Top heavy company

Not great growth paths and opportunities for non-technical, non-engineer folks; health plans are all high deductible and poor for such a large company, promote a "speak up" culture but HR doesn't do a great job at creating psychological safe environments, some of the older benefits (remote and hybrid work) are going away Great growth for engineers, not so much for others
Lots of beurocracy, as with any large company Excellent company, badass products

Very in-group/out-group. You will know if you're with the in-crowd who get promotions/higher pay rises or if you're ignored. You can heroically push yourself to extremes in terms of high-skill work and number of hours working and still it's never enough to please middle management. Promotion for out-group people is literally down to years of service alone. There's a strong sense of hiding negatives or problems in fa...
nothing really, can't think of anything nice place
Union/Salary tension, Some departments have poor communication. Level 1
Isolating work environment for any new hire. Isolation
poor communication and cohesion among sister disciplines its ok
Salary is not competitive. A lot of reorgs Review
Poor communication and resource management Special Test Equipment GSE Electronic Engineer
